ORDER DENYING PETITION FOR PEREMPTORY WRIT OF MANDAMUS

LAW & MOTION, DEPT. 302, AS TO THE APR-12-2022 HEARING RE: PETITIONER MARY CARDER'S MOTION FOR PEREMPTORY WRIT OF MANDAMUS, THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. PETITIONER MARY CARDER'S MOTION FOR PEREMPTORY WRIT OF MANDAMUS IS DENIED. (SEE WORD MINUTES FOR COMPLETE RULING.) ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER JR.; CLERK: SEAN KANE; COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR NO. 8600, MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM (302/RBU)

LAW & MOTION, DEPT. 302, AS TO THE APR-12-2022 HEARING RE: PETITIONER MARY CARDER'S MOTION FOR ORDER GRANTING FIRST AMENDMENT TO PETITION FOR PEREMPTORY WRIT OF MANDAMUS, THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. PETITIONER MARY CARDER'S UNOPPOSED MOTION FOR ORDER GRANTING FIRST AMENDMENT TO PETITION FOR PEREMPTORY WRIT OF MANDAMUS IS GRANTED. COUNSEL FOR THE PREVAILING PARTY IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH STATES THAT THE MOTION WAS NOT OPPOSED. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER JR.; CLERK: SEAN KANE; COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR NO. 8600, MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM (302/RBU)
LAW & MOTION, DEPT. 302, MOTION FOR ORDER GRANTING FIRST AMENDMENT TO PETITION FOR PEREMPTORY WRIT OF MANDAMUS CCP 473(A), 576)) IS OFF CALENDAR AS A DUPLICATE ENTRY. (302/RBU)

Motion For Order Granting First Amendment To Petition For Peremptory Writ Of Mandamus And Points And Authorities Iso.(Ccp473(A), 576)
Off Calendar Apr-12-2022 Off Calendar

Motion For Peremptory Writ Of Mandamus, Points And Authorities In Support Of
Off Calendar Apr-12-2022 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: PETITIONER MARY CARDER's Motion For Peremptory Writ Of Mandamus.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Petitioner Mary Carder's Motion for Peremptory Writ of Mandamus is denied.

(See Word minutes for complete ruling.)

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/RBU)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Granting First Amendment To Petition For Peremptory Writ Of Mandamus And Points And Authorities Iso.(Ccp 473(A), 576)
Off Calendar Apr-12-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: PETITIONER MARY CARDER's Motion For Order Granting First Amendment To Petition For Peremptory Writ Of Mandamus.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Petitioner Mary Carder's unopposed motion for order granting first amendment to petition for peremptory writ of mandamus is granted.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare and submit a proposed order which states that the motion was not opposed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/RBU)
